findings.title: Fair-tier label color is #b45309, not the spec AC7-named #b7710d (intentional, floor-F8-mandated)
findings.note: AC7 enumerated #b7710d for Fair, but it computes to 3.90:1 on white — below WCAG-AA 4.5:1. Floor F8 (non-disarmable) requires >=4.5:1, so the build correctly uses #b45309 (5.02:1). The deviation from the literal spec hex is intentional and floor-mandated; the spec's hex was the error. Weak (#c0392b, 5.44:1) and Strong (#1a7a3c, 5.39:1) match the spec. Accepted/intentional — no code change.

findings.title: count===3 (length>=8) -> Fair boundary lacked a dedicated test
findings.note: Fair was reached only via count=4 and count=5-but-short; the count=3 sub-path (e.g. 'abc12345') was untested, leaving the count<3 Weak/Fair boundary partly unguarded. RESOLVED in a P5 micro-cycle: added evaluate('abc12345')->Fair boundary test; suite now 19/19 green.

summary: lens:engine compute-only review of the 4-file diff (591 LOC) against spec.md/plan.md. correctness, plan-adherence, risk-classify, and principles(simplify): 0 findings. spec-adherence: 1 LOW (AC7 Fair color is an intentional F8-mandated correction #b7710d->#b45309). test-gaps: 1 MEDIUM (count=3 Fair boundary), RESOLVED in a P5 micro-cycle (suite 19/19). Finder set: correctness, spec-adherence, plan-adherence, test-gaps, risk-classify, matali principles. The pr-review-toolkit adapter tier was not separately dispatched (overlapping with correctness/test-gaps on a tiny vanilla-JS diff already covered by P4 behavioral verification). No blocking findings.
